items.hook: Michael Inside is a 2017 Irish prison film written, directed and co-produced by Frank Berry.
items.body: It was nominated for four Irish Film & Television Awards and won the Best Film prize. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Brutus, also called Brutus the Bear, was a grizzly bear who was adopted as a newborn cub by the naturalist Casey Anderson, star of the National Geographic documentary Expedition Grizzly.
items.body: Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Gala Aleksić is a Serbian actress who gained fame by appearing in some of the most popular films, TV shows, and TV commercials in the mid-1980s.
items.body: She is married to TV director Vladimir Aleksić; they have two sons together, Demian and Pavle. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Londner, and her stage partner and husband Henri Gerro, were nicknamed "the wandering stars of vaudeville and the Jewish music hall."
items.body: Rosita Londner was an Argentine actress and singer, with a popular career in Jewish theater and Jewish music. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Words on Bathroom Walls is a 2020 American coming-of-age romantic drama film directed by Thor Freudenthal and written by Nick Naveda, based on the novel of the same name by Julia Walton.
items.body: The film stars Charlie Plummer, Andy García, Taylor Russell, AnnaSophia Robb, Beth Grant, Molly Parker and Walton Goggins. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Michener about the Pacific campaign in World War II.
items.body: Tales of the South Pacific is a Pulitzer Prize-winning collection of sequentially related short stories by James A. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Feminism in the United States is often divided chronologically into first-wave, second-wave, third-wave, and fourth-wave feminism.
items.body: Feminism is aimed at defining, establishing, and defending a state of equal political, economic, cultural, and social rights for women. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
